instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Are the Benefits of Buying Term Insurance with Return of Premium?
A term insurance policy is a pure protection policy. The main objective of the plan is financial security against premature death. If the insured dies during the policy tenure, the term plan gives financial assistance to the bereaved family. This helps the family cope with the financial loss suffered in the absence of the breadwinner.
Given that term plans are protection-oriented, many plans do not pay a benefit on maturity. This means that if you survive the policy tenure, you don’t get any benefit. A benefit is paid only in the case of premature death.
Many individuals consider this to be a drawback of term insurance. They believe that they would end up losing the premiums if they were to survive the policy tenure. Though the plan is meant for protection, many policyholders expect a return from the plan.
To address the need for a return, a term plan with return of premium option has been designed. Let’s understand what the plan is all about.
What is a term plan with return of premium option?
A term insurance plan with a return of premium is one that has a maturity benefit. The plan covers the risk of premature death during the policy tenure. If the insured survives the tenure and the policy matures, the plan refunds the premiums paid.
Thus, the term insurance plan with return of premium offers security against premature death as well as the guarantee of premium refunds shall the plan mature.
How does the return of premium plans prove to be beneficial?
Return of premium plans is a step above basic term plans that do not have a maturity benefit. Here are the benefits that term return of premium plans provide –
Guaranteed maturity benefit
The primary difference that the return of premium plans has against basic term plans is the maturity benefit. Term plan with return of premium option promise the refund of premiums on maturity. This gives you a win-win option. You get coverage throughout the policy tenure, which provides financial security. Moreover, after the tenure expires, you are assured of a guaranteed corpus that refunds the premiums that you paid for the coverage. So, while you pay for the coverage, you get the payment back if you survive. What could be better than this?
Comprehensive coverage
When it comes to coverage, terms with the return of premium plans do not shirk away. They provide a comprehensive scope of coverage against premature death. You can opt for a high sum assured and provide financial security for your family. The plan also has optional riders that allow you to enhance the scope of protection at minimal premiums.
Thus, you can avail of a comprehensive scope of coverage, complete with riders, and make a plan that comes to your and your family’s financial aid in the case of emergencies.
Affordable
These plans have affordable premiums making it possible for you to opt for an adequate sum assured without hurting your pockets. Moreover, you can choose from different payment modes like regular premium, limited premium or single premium. This ensures that the plan suits your budget. You can use the term insurance premium calculator to estimate the premium and assess its affordability. Pay the premium annually or in installments over six months, quarterly, or monthly based on your suitability.
Tax benefits:
Like other life insurance plans, term with return of premium plans also offers tax benefits. The premium paid is eligible for deduction under Section 80C. You can claim up to Rs.1.5 lakhs of deduction on premiums up to 10% of the sum assured.
The death benefit received is completely tax-free for your family.
Even on maturity, the premiums that are refunded are tax-free in your hands under Section 10(10D), provided the premium was up to 10% of the sum assured1.
These tax benefits allow you to lower your tax liability when you buy the plan and get tax-free benefits on death or maturity.
Financial protection under term with return of premium
Being a term insurance plan, there is no dearth of financial protection under the return of premium plans. You can –
Choose a high sum assured adequate enough to provide financial security to your family. There’s usually no limit on the sum assured.
Get plans with inbuilt riders for enhanced protection.
Get optional riders to widen the coverage further.
So, with a term return of premium plan, you can get complete financial protection for emergencies.
Conclusion
The premium for the policy is slightly on the higher side given the maturity benefit that it offers. However, the plan is affordable. You can use the term insurance premium calculator to assess the premium when buying the coverage.
If you are looking for protection and a maturity benefit, a term with a return of premium can be a good choice. Besides offering complete coverage, return of premium plans also offer a maturity benefit giving you protection as well as savings in one.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. output.final_text:
What Are the Benefits of Buying Term Insurance with Return of Premium?
A term insurance policy is a pure protection policy. The main objective of the plan is financial security against premature death. If the insured dies during the policy tenure, the term plan gives financial assistance to the bereaved family, provided all due premiums are paid. This helps the family cope with the financial loss suffered in the absence of the breadwinner.
Given that term plans are protection-oriented, many plans do not pay a benefit on maturity. This means that if you survive the policy tenure, you don’t get any benefit. A benefit is paid only in the case of premature death.
Though the plan is meant for protection, many policyholders expect a return from the plan.
To address the need for a return, a term plan with return of premium option has been designed. Let’s understand what the plan is all about.
What is a term plan with return of premium option?
A term insurance plan with a return of premium is one that has a maturity benefit. The plan covers the risk of premature death during the policy tenure. If the insured survives the tenure and the policy matures, the plan returns the premiums paid subject to the terms & conditions of the policy.
Thus, the term insurance plan with return of premium offers financial security against premature death as well as the premium refunds, shall the plan mature.
How does the return of premium plans prove to be beneficial?
Here are some benefits that term plans with return of premium option provide –
1. maturity benefit
The primary difference that the return of premium plans has against basic term plans is the maturity benefit. Term plan with return of premium option promise the return of premiums on maturity. This gives you a win-win option. You get coverage throughout the policy tenure, which provides financial security. Moreover, after the tenure expires, you are assured of a corpus that returns the premiums that you paid for the coverage as per terms & conditions of the policy. So, while you pay for the coverage, you get the payment back if you survive. What could be better than this? 2. Comprehensive coverage
When it comes to coverage, terms with the return of premium plans do not shirk away. They provide a comprehensive scope of coverage against premature death. You can opt for a high sum assured and provide financial security for your family. The plans also have optional riders that allow you to enhance the scope of protection at minimal extra premiums. Thus, you can avail of a comprehensive scope of coverage, complete with riders, and make a plan that comes to your and your family’s financial aid in the case of emergencies. 3. Affordable
These plans have affordable premiums making it possible for you to opt for an adequate sum assured. Moreover, you can choose from different payment modes like regular premium, limited premium or single premium. This ensures that the plan suits your budget.
You can use the term insurance premium calculator to estimate the premium and assess its affordability. You may choose to Pay the premium annually or in instalments over six months, quarterly, or monthly based on your suitability. 4. Tax benefits:
Like other life insurance plans, term with return of premium plans also offers tax benefits. The premium paid is eligible for deduction under Section 80C of Income Tax Act 1961 (the Act). You can claim up to Rs.1.5 lakhs of deduction on premiums up to 10% of the sum assured. The death benefit received is completely tax-free for your nominee under Section 10(10D) of the Act. Even on maturity, the premiums that are returned are tax-free in your hands under Section 10(10D) of the Act, provided the premium was up to 10% of the sum assured1. The said deductions and exemptions are subject to the provisions contained in the Act, as amended from time to time. These tax benefits allow you to lower your tax liability when you buy the plan and get tax-free benefits on death or maturity.
Conclusion
The premium for the policy is slightly on the higher side given the maturity benefit that it offers. You can use the term insurance premium calculator to estimate the amount of premium when buying the coverage.
If you are looking for protection and a maturity benefit, a term insurance with a return of premium can be a preferred choice. Besides offering complete coverage, return of premium plans also offer a maturity benefit giving you protection as well as savings in one instrument.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
